last_frame_analysis:
Frame 211
Setting/Scene:
The scene is a static title card on a solid black background. The layout features white text centered horizontally. To the left is the phrase "Distributed by" in a standard sans-serif font. To the right is the "BBC STUDIOS" logo, where the letters "B", "B", and "C" are individually enclosed in white squares, followed by the word "STUDIOS" in bold, uppercase letters.

Action/Movement:
There is no movement or action in the text elements; it is a static credit screen.

New Information:
- The text explicitly states the distribution partner: "Distributed by BBC STUDIOS".
- The visual focus shifts to the BBC Studios logo, replacing the previous production company logos (Fulcrum Media, Screen Queensland, ABC).
- Unlike the previous frames (Frames 207-210), the character Bluey and the colorful illustrations are not visible in this specific frame.

Continuity Points:
- This frame follows the previous credit screens (Frames 208-210) which listed the executive producers, series director, post-production funding, and broadcaster association.
- The narrative context from the transcript (discussing the boomerang, the fridge, and Dad's encouragement) is now fully concluded as the video transitions to the final distribution credit.
- The appearance of the BBC Studios logo signifies the final entity responsible for the show's distribution, serving as the closing visual anchor for the end credits.

Key continuation points:
- Note the specific role listed (Distribution) to understand the final step in the production chain.
- Observe the BBC Studios logo as the final visual anchor for the end credits.
- Confirm the absence of character elements, marking the definitive end of the credit sequence.
